w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: IoT Smart Bin Monitoring System - Complete Documentation
- Initializes WiFi connection using stored SSID and password.
- Ensures device is connected before sending any data.
- Turns off WiFi module completely using WIFI_OFF mode.
- Reduces power consumption significantly in battery mode.
- Sends trigger pulse to ultrasonic sensor.
- Converts time to distance in centimeters.
- Repeats measurement 5 times and averages result.
- Reads both ultrasonic sensors for bin level.
- Reads HX711 load cell raw values for weight.
- Reads battery voltage using ADC and voltage divider.
- Reads current and power from INA219 sensor.
- Establishes WiFi connection if not connected.
- Sends HTTP POST request to server.
- Disconnects WiFi if in battery mode.
- Sends only battery voltage in JSON format.
- Activated when solar power and current exceed threshold.
- Full data is sent every 1 minute.
- Activated when no solar input is detected.
- LCD displays "BATTERY MODE" briefly then turns OFF.
- WiFi is turned OFF to conserve energy.
- Full system data is sent every 20 minutes.
- Independent system that sends battery voltage every 1 minute.
- Works in both solar and battery mode.
- If threshold exceeded, triggers immediate data transmission.
- Sends another update when gas returns to normal.
- Uses HTTPS protocol for secure communication.
- Includes device ID and sensor readings.
- WiFi is only enabled when required in battery mode.
- WiFi is disabled after sending data.
- Reduces power consumption and prevents brownout.
- Solar mode shows real-time electrical values.
- Battery mode disables LCD to save power.
- Provides user feedback on system state.
- Ultrasonic sensors for bin level measurement.
- HX711 load cells for weight detection.
- MQ gas sensors for environmental monitoring.
- INA219 for current and power measurement.
- Automatically switches between solar and battery modes.
